mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that lowers discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 310 indicates elevated inflation, prompting central bank tightening and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns has a Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS > 150,000) indicate a robust labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 4%) signals economic weakness, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns during periods of elevated unemployment.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding supply, boosting S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO's predictive power for SPX returns has a Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and UNRATE indicate monetary tightening and labor market stress, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE on SPX returns has a Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) reflect robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of HOUST and RSAFS on SPX returns has a Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
